title: Apache Spark Engineering
description: A consolidated, comprehensive roadmap for learning Apache Spark end to end — merging distributed systems fundamentals, core and structured APIs, the SQL engine, modern lakehouse and storage patterns, streaming, performance tuning, deployment, and production operations.

phases.name: Foundations
phases.description: How Spark distributes work across machines and gets an application running.

sections.name: Distributed Computing Fundamentals
sections.description: The core building blocks of how Spark distributes work across a cluster.
topics: Cluster manager concepts (YARN, Mesos, Standalone, Kubernetes)

sections.name: Cluster Architecture & Execution Lifecycle
sections.description: How a Spark application is broken down, scheduled, and executed.

sections.name: Spark Core Fundamentals
sections.description: Getting a Spark application up, configured, and observable.
topics: spark-submit flags and master URL formats

phases.name: Core & Structured APIs
phases.description: The programming interfaces used to express computation in Spark.

sections.name: Resilient Distributed Datasets (RDDs)
sections.description: The low-level, fault-tolerant distributed collection API underlying all of Spark.
topics: Recovering failed tasks and executor loss mitigation

sections.name: DataFrame & Dataset APIs
sections.description: The structured, higher-level APIs used for most modern Spark development.

sections.name: Window Functions
sections.description: Ranking, aggregate, and analytic functions computed over row windows.

sections.name: Joins & Relational Operations
sections.description: How Spark combines datasets, and the strategies it picks between.
topics: Inner, outer, semi, and anti joins

sections.name: Advanced RDD & DataFrame Transformations
sections.description: Less common but important transformation patterns.

sections.name: User Defined Functions (UDFs)
sections.description: Extending Spark SQL with custom logic, and its performance implications.
topics: Built-in functions versus custom UDFs (performance tradeoffs)

phases.name: Spark SQL Engine & Optimization
phases.description: How Spark parses, plans, optimizes, and executes queries under the hood.

sections.name: Spark SQL Engine & Catalyst Optimizer
sections.description: The query planner and optimizer at the heart of Spark SQL.
topics: Spark Thrift Server (JDBC/ODBC access for BI tools)

phases.name: Data & Storage
phases.description: File formats, connectors, catalogs, and lakehouse table formats.

sections.name: File Formats & Storage Optimization
sections.description: Choosing and tuning the on-disk formats Spark reads and writes.
topics: Small file problem and compaction strategies

sections.name: Cloud Storage & Database Connectors
sections.description: Reading from and writing to object stores and relational databases.
topics: S3A implementation and file path patterns
Partitioned database reads and write batching
Credential and secrets management for connectors
Kafka and REST APIs as batch data sources

sections.name: NoSQL Database Integration
sections.description: Connecting Spark to Cassandra, MongoDB, and similar stores.
topics: Schema and type conversion across NoSQL stores

sections.name: Metastore & Catalog Management
sections.description: Tracking table metadata and schemas across a data platform.

sections.name: Lakehouse Table Formats
sections.description: ACID transactional layers on top of data lake storage.
topics: Delta Lake ACID transactions and transaction log
Apache Iceberg architecture and partition evolution
Apache Hudi upsert and incremental processing semantics
MERGE INTO syntax and upsert patterns

phases.name: Streaming & Data Quality
phases.description: Processing unbounded data and validating data correctness.

sections.name: Structured Streaming
sections.description: Processing unbounded data with the same DataFrame API as batch.
topics: Kafka source integration and offset management
Stateful stream processing and state stores

sections.name: Data Quality Engineering
sections.description: Validating, testing, and monitoring the correctness of data itself.

phases.name: Machine Learning & Graph Processing
phases.description: Spark's libraries for large-scale ML and graph analytics.

sections.name: Machine Learning & Graph Processing
sections.description: MLlib and GraphFrames for analytics beyond ETL.
topics: Feature engineering at scale (VectorAssembler, StringIndexer, etc.)
Graph algorithms (PageRank, connected components, shortest paths)

phases.name: Performance & Scaling
phases.description: Making Spark jobs faster, cheaper, and more stable.

sections.name: Performance Tuning & Memory Management
sections.description: Diagnosing and fixing slow, unstable, or expensive Spark jobs.
topics: Execution memory versus storage memory (unified memory model)
Garbage collection tuning (G1GC vs. Parallel GC)
Memory overhead limits and OOM troubleshooting
Data skew mitigation and salting techniques
Task concurrency and core allocation tuning

phases.name: Deployment & Infrastructure
phases.description: Running Spark on self-hosted clusters or managed cloud platforms.

sections.name: Cluster Deployment & Environment Setup
sections.description: Running Spark on different cluster managers and packaging it to ship.

sections.name: Cloud & Managed Platforms
sections.description: Running Spark on managed services instead of self-hosted clusters.
topics: Databricks runtime and Photon execution engine
Instance type selection and spot/preemptible instances

phases.name: Engineering Practices & Operations
phases.description: Treating Spark jobs like production software: tested, monitored, and orchestrated.

sections.name: Application Testing & Development Practices
sections.description: Treating Spark jobs like proper software with tests and pipelines.
topics: CI/CD pipeline automation for Spark jobs

sections.name: Production Monitoring & Observability
sections.description: Knowing what your Spark jobs are doing once they're live.
topics: Spark UI analysis (jobs, stages, SQL tab)
Event log parsing and history server

sections.name: Workflow Orchestration
sections.description: Scheduling and sequencing Spark jobs as part of larger pipelines.
topics: Pipeline retries and failure recovery strategies

phases.name: Administration & Governance
phases.description: Securing and maintaining a Spark platform over time.

sections.name: Security Hardening & Governance
sections.description: Protecting data and controlling access across a Spark platform.
topics: SSL/TLS encryption for data in transit

sections.name: Maintenance & Administration
sections.description: The ongoing operational work of keeping a Spark platform healthy.
